articles.title: WHO Chief Visits Epicenter Of Ebola Outbreak As Virus Spreads Faster Than The Response
articles.content:
布尼亚（刚果民主共和国）消息——世界卫生组织总干事周六访问了刚果东部城市布尼亚，这座城市正处于一种罕见埃博拉病毒暴发的中心地带。尽管卫生设施组织得更好，新的援助也陆续抵达，但病毒传播速度仍超过了应对措施。

世卫组织总干事谭德塞强调了建立社区信任、确保安全埋葬以防止疫情蔓延的重要性，并敦促各国重新考虑旅行禁令和对边境关闭的做法，称这些措施“会抑制透明度”。

“刚果民主共和国此前曾经历过16次埃博拉疫情，每次都成功终结。这是第17次。这段历史让我充满信心，”谭德塞周六在与刚果卫生部长举行的新闻发布会上表示。

世卫组织周五公布的最新官方数据显示，目前有906例疑似病例和223例疑似死亡病例。邻国乌干达卫生部周五表示，该国已确认9例病例和1例死亡。

目前的埃博拉病毒类型为邦文迪布里奥病毒，目前尚无经批准的特效药或疫苗。

欧盟捐赠的医疗援助已于周四运抵位于伊图里省的布尼亚，预计未来几天将有更多物资运抵。美国同日宣布追加8000万美元援助，使美国承诺援助总额超过1.12亿美元。

据美联社记者报道，布尼亚 Rwampara 医院和总医院的应对工作看起来更有组织性，增加了工作人员、防护装备和医疗用品，但患者仍昼夜不停地涌入。

无国界医生组织周六警告说，应对工作未能跟上这次疫情的传播速度，这是有记录以来传播最快的疫情之一。

“此前从未有埃博拉疫情在宣布暴发后如此短的时间内出现这么多病例，”无国界医生组织行动副主任艾伦·冈萨雷斯博士在一份声明中表示。“没有人知道这次疫情的真正规模和严重程度。”

冈萨雷斯呼吁立即扩大检测范围，加快部署援助人员，并确保医疗物资的持续供应。

由于当地居民对处理患者遗体严格医疗协议的不满情绪日益高涨，这与当地葬礼习俗发生冲突，医护人员面临的危险加剧。居民已对医疗中心发动了至少三次袭击。

“我们来这里不是告诉人们该怎么做，而是来倾听，”谭德塞周六表示。“建立信任需要时间，而信任始于倾听。”

“我理解失去亲人的痛苦，也理解妥善安葬对他们的意义，但某些做法，包括触摸埃博拉死亡者的遗体，可能会进一步传播病毒，”谭德塞说。

伊图里省民主同盟军（与伊斯兰国组织有关联的叛军组织）以及一个族裔民兵联盟的袭击也阻碍了应对工作。该疾病还出现在伊图里省以南的北基伍省和南基伍省，卢旺达支持的M23叛军组织控制着包括戈马和布卡武在内的多个关键城市。叛军方面已报告了两例病例。

乌干达和卢旺达已关闭边境，而特朗普政府上周禁止最近访问过刚果、乌干达或南苏丹的非美国护照持有者入境。

“我还要请那些实施旅行禁令或关闭边境的国家重新考虑，”谭德塞说，并表示这些措施“会抑制拯救生命的透明度”。

——记者班舒罗来自塞内加尔达喀尔。德国波恩的美联社记者萨莱赫·姆瓦纳米隆戈也对此报道有贡献。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

BUNIA, Congo (AP) — The head of the World Health Organization on Saturday visited eastern Congo’s Bunia, a city at the heart of an outbreak of a rare type of Ebola, where the virus is spreading faster than the response despite better-organized health facilities and new aid arrivals.

WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus stressed the importance of building community trust, ensuring safe burials to prevent the spread of the outbreak and urged countries to reconsider travel bans and border closures, saying they “discourage transparency.”

Advertisement

“The Democratic Republic of Congo has faced Ebola before, 16 times, and has ended every outbreak. This is the 17th. That history gives me real confidence,” Tedros said during a news conference Saturday alongside Congo’s health minister.

The health organization said Friday latest official figures showed 906 suspected cases and 223 suspected deaths. Neighboring Uganda has confirmed nine cases and one death, the Ugandan Health Ministry said Friday.

Advertisement

The Bundibugyo virus, the current species of Ebola, has no approved treatment or vaccine.

Medical aid donated by the European Union arrived in Bunia in Ituri province on Thursday. More shipments are expected in the coming days. The U.S. announced $80 million in additional aid on the same day, bringing its total commitment to more than $112 million.

Response efforts at Bunia’s Rwampara and General hospitals appeared more organized, with additional staff, protective gear and medical supplies, though patients continue arriving around the clock, according to an Associated Press reporter.

The response has not kept pace with one of the fastest-spreading outbreaks on record, Doctors Without Borders, or MSF, warned on Saturday.

Advertisement

“Never before has an Ebola outbreak recorded so many cases so soon after its declaration,” Dr. Alan Gonzalez, MSF’s deputy director of operations, said in a statement. “Nobody knows the true scale and severity of this outbreak.”

Gonzalez called for an immediate expansion of testing, faster deployment of aid workers and sustained access for medical supplies.

Advertisement

The dangers faced by health workers have been heightened by anger among residents over the stringent medical protocols for handling the victims’ bodies, which clash with local burial rites. Residents have launched at least three attacks against health centers.

“We are not here to tell people what to do, we are here to listen,” Tedros said Saturday. “Building trust takes time, and it starts with listening.”

“I understand how painful it is to lose someone, and how much it means to honor them properly, but certain practices, including touching the bodies of those who have died from Ebola, can spread the virus further,” Tedros said.

Advertisement

Attacks in Ituri by the Allied Democratic Forces, a rebel group allied with the Islamic State group, and a coalition of ethnic militias have also hindered the response. The illness also has been reported in the Congolese provinces of North Kivu and South Kivu, south of Ituri, where the Rwanda-backed M23 rebel group controls many key cities, including Goma and Bukavu. The rebels have reported two cases.

Uganda and Rwanda have closed their borders, while the Trump administration last week banned entry of non-U.S. passport holders who had recently visited Congo, Uganda or South Sudan.

“I would also ask countries that have imposed travel bans or border closures to reconsider,” Tedros said, saying such measures “discourage the transparency that saves lives.”

Advertisement

——

Banchereau reported from Dakar, Senegal. Associated Press writer Saleh Mwanamilongo in Bonn, Germany, contributed to this report.

Related
world newsHealth NewsWorld Health OrganizationebolaCongo

articles.title: 5 Rescued After Being Trapped In Flooded Laos Cave For 10 Days, 2 Still Missing
articles.content:
曼谷（美联社）——老挝救援人员周六表示，他们已安全疏散了四名被困在洪水淹没洞穴中10天的村民，此前一天，另一名被困者已被成功救出。仍有两人失踪。

老挝和泰国的救援组织在社交媒体上发布了这次成功行动的消息，并附上了这些男子躺在担架上、戴着氧气面罩、裹着铝箔毯的照片。

据报道，这些村民上周进入洞穴寻找有价值的矿物，随后被阻挡出口的暴发洪水困住。另一名村民及时逃出，并向当局报告了剩余七人的情况。

老挝组织“人民救援志愿者”在其Facebook页面上表示，洞穴内的水位已下降，足够让被困者与进入洞穴运送食物和水的潜水员一起离开。他们表示将继续搜寻另外两人。

一名在现场的泰国救援人员查基特·坦唐在网上发布了一段视频，显示这些男子被一个接一个地搀扶着走出洞穴入口，身上满是泥巴。他们出来后，一些人瘫倒在地，被一群喜极而泣的救援人员拥抱。

被困人员中的第一人于周五安全撤离。据救援人员称，那次行动耗时约30分钟。视频显示，他与一名潜水员一起从水中浮出水面，喘着气，然后艰难地爬过一条狭窄的洪水通道，摇摇晃晃地站起来。

其中五人在周三被发现活着。他们的名字分别是坎拉、穆德、伊、英和兰。

老挝和邻国泰国的救援队得到了日本和马来西亚同事的协助。据报道，印度尼西亚、法国和澳大利亚的专家也抵达了现场。该地位于中部省份赛宋奔的偏远地区，距首都万象以北约120公里（75英里）。

其中几名救援人员曾参与过2018年泰国北部12名学生和他们的足球教练的复杂洞穴救援行动。

救援人员也在准备搜寻另外两名失踪的村民。

泰国救援组织“梅塔坦姆救援加莱辛”负责人肯加吉·邦卡旺周五表示，救援队计划探索洞穴更深处，在发现幸存者的地方再深入约20至25米（码）。然而，他警告说，该区域洪水严重。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

BANGKOK (AP) — Rescue workers in Laos said Saturday they have safely evacuated four villagers trapped in a flooded cave for 10 days, the day after another one was successfully extracted. Two men remain missing.

Lao and Thai rescue groups posted about the successful operation on social media, along with photos of the men lying on stretchers, wearing oxygen masks and being wrapped in foil blankets.

Advertisement

The villagers had reportedly entered the cave last week to look for valuable minerals before being trapped by flash flooding that blocked their way out. One other villager escaped in time and alerted the authorities to the seven left behind.

Lao organization Rescue Volunteer for People said on its Facebook page that the water level inside the cave receded low enough for them to leave with divers who had gone in to deliver food and water. They said they will continue their search for the two who remain missing.

Advertisement

A video posted online by a Thai rescuer at the scene, Chakkit Taengtang, showed the men being assisted one by one out of the cave’s entrance, bodies covered in mud. After they got out, some collapsed on the ground and were hugged by a group of rescuers who cried in joy.

The first of the trapped group was safely evacuated on Friday. According to rescuers, that operation took about 30 minutes. Videos showed the moment he emerged from the water alongside a diver, catching his breath before struggling to crawl through a narrow, flooded passage and rising unsteadily to his feet.

Advertisement

The villagers had reportedly entered the cave last week to look for valuable minerals before being trapped by flash flooding that blocked their way out. One other villager escaped in time and alerted the authorities to the seven left behind.

Five of them were found alive Wednesday. They were identified by their first names as Khamla, Mued, Ee, Ing and Laen.

Rescue teams from Laos and neighboring Thailand were joined by Japanese and Malaysian colleagues. Indonesian, French and Australian specialists also reportedly arrived at the site in a rugged area in the central province of Xaisomboun, about 120 kilometers (75 miles) north of the capital, Vientiane.

Advertisement

Several of them had taken part in the complicated 2018 cave rescue in northern Thailand of 12 schoolboys and their soccer coach.

Rescuers are also preparing to search for the two villagers who remain missing.

Kengkaj Bongkawong, head of the Thai rescue group Metta Tham Rescue Kalasin, said Friday that the team plans to explore an area deeper inside the cave, about 20 to 25 meters (yards) beyond where the survivors were found. However, he cautioned that the section is heavily flooded.

Advertisement

Related

laos

5 Villagers Missing In A Flooded Laos Cave For More Than A Week Have Been Found Alive, Rescuers Say

articles.title: U.S. Commander Meets With Cuban Military Officials As Trump Pressures Island Nation
articles.content:
华盛顿（美联社）——美国拉丁美洲最高指挥官星期五在关塔那摩美国海军基地附近与古巴军方领导人进行了“关于行动安全的简短交流”，这是美国总统唐纳德·特朗普加大对古巴领导人施压以来最新一位访问该岛的美国官员。

特朗普曾警告称，在美军于一次一月突袭行动中俘获委内瑞拉独裁领导人尼古拉斯·马杜罗之后，古巴“将是下一个”。数月来，特朗普政府已对古巴实施石油封锁，在加勒比海部署军舰，并以联邦罪名起诉古巴前总统劳尔·卡斯特罗。

美国南方司令部司令弗朗西斯·多诺万将军会见了古巴武装部队副总司令罗伯托·莱格拉·索托隆戈中将及其他古巴军方官员。

古巴武装力量在一份声明中表示，双方“积极看待此次会晤，因为会议涉及分隔军事飞地的周边安全问题，双方同意保持两军指挥部之间的沟通”。

包括国务卿马尔科·鲁比奥和中央情报局局长约翰·拉特克利夫在内的特朗普高级助手也曾与古巴官员会面，探索改善关系的可能性。但美方从这些会谈中并未获得满意结果，导致对古巴政府实施更多制裁。

除此次会晤外，多诺万还评估了关塔那摩美国海军基地的安全状况，并与基地官员讨论了“军人及其家属的安全问题以及作战准备状态”，美国南方司令部在社交媒体X上表示。

尽管与古巴社会主义领导人存在数十年摩擦，美国仍维持该基地运营。特朗普希望推翻这些古巴领导人。

美国在加勒比海部署了少量海军舰艇，包括至少一艘两栖攻击舰，规模远小于马杜罗突袭行动当时的兵力。

星期五，五角大楼宣布，一支由1300名水手和海军陆战队组成的新部队将接替去年夏天部署到该地区的第22海军远征队。

——哈瓦那美联社记者安德烈亚·罗德里格斯对此报道亦有贡献
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

WASHINGTON (AP) — The top U.S. commander in Latin America met with Cuban military leaders Friday in a “brief exchange on operational security matters” near the U.S. Navy base in Guantanamo Bay, the latest official to visit the island nation as President Donald Trump ramps up pressure on its leaders.

Trump has warned that Cuba “is next” after U.S. military forces captured Venezuela’s autocratic leader, Nicolás Maduro, in a January raid. In the months since, the Trump administration has imposed an oil blockade on Cuba, maintained warships in the Caribbean Sea and indicted former Cuban President Raúl Castro on federal charges.

Advertisement

Gen. Francis Donovan, head of U.S. Southern Command, met with Lt. Gen. Roberto Legrá Sotolongo and other Cuban military officials.

Cuba’s Revolutionary Armed Forces said in a statement that both sides viewed “the meeting positively because it addressed security issues along the perimeter separating the military enclave, and they agreed to maintain communication between the two military commands.”

Top Trump aides, including Secretary of State Marco Rubio and CIA chief John Ratcliffe, also have met with Cuban officials to explore possible improvements in relations. But the U.S. side has come away unimpressed from those talks, leading to even more sanctions imposed on the Cuban government.

Advertisement

Besides the meeting, Donovan also assessed the security of the U.S. naval base in Guantanamo Bay and discussed the “safety of service members and their families, and operational readiness with base officials,” U.S. Southern Command said in a post on X.

The U.S. maintains the base despite decades of friction with Cuba’s socialist leaders, whom Trump wants removed from power.

Advertisement

The U.S. military has a handful of Navy ships, including at least one amphibious assault ship, in the Caribbean, a much smaller force than was present at the time of the Maduro raid.

On Friday, the Pentagon announced that a new unit of 1,300 sailors and Marines would be replacing the 22nd Marine Expeditionary Unit, which deployed to the region last summer.

___

Associated Press writer Andrea Rodríguez in Havana contributed to this report.

Advertisement
